programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP42: CHAMPIONS SPECIAL WITH CURTIS JONES
programme.sub-title: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.
programme.desc: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP52: CHAMPIONS STORY
programme.sub-title: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United’s record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides—Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.
programme.desc: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United’s record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP48: (Subbed) REGGAE RHYTHMS IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE
programme.sub-title: Reggae Rhythms in the Premier League explores the powerful connection between Jamaica and the world’s most-watched football league, showcasing how the island’s culture, music, and style have influenced the Premier League. We feature iconic Jamaican players who have left their mark, while also diving into the impact of reggae music, fashion, and fan culture on the game. Through exclusive interviews and untold stories, we see how Jamaican heritage has shaped both football and broader global culture, from the pitch to the streets.
programme.desc: Reggae Rhythms in the Premier League explores the powerful connection between Jamaica and the world’s most-watched football league, showcasing how the island’s culture, music, and style have influenced the Premier League. We feature iconic Jamaican players who have left their mark, while also diving into the impact of reggae music, fashion, and fan culture on the game. Through exclusive interviews and untold stories, we see how Jamaican heritage has shaped both football and broader global culture, from the pitch to the streets.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP49: INSIDE MATTERS
programme.sub-title: In this powerful and deeply personal edition of PL Stories, former Premier League footballer and mental health advocate Clarke Carlisle sits down with players past and present to explore the hidden struggles behind life at the top of the game. From the pressures of expectation to the pain of isolation, Jason McAteer, Phil Jones, Eberechi Eze and Steven Caulker open up about their journeys with mental health—sharing raw, honest accounts of their lowest moments and the strength it took to rise again.
programme.desc: In this powerful and deeply personal edition of PL Stories, former Premier League footballer and mental health advocate Clarke Carlisle sits down with players past and present to explore the hidden struggles behind life at the top of the game. From the pressures of expectation to the pain of isolation, Jason McAteer, Phil Jones, Eberechi Eze and Steven Caulker open up about their journeys with mental health—sharing raw, honest accounts of their lowest moments and the strength it took to rise again.

With insight, anecdotes and tough decisions, he builds his dream team from his unforgettable years in the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP46: (Subbed) PHILIPPE COUTINHO
programme.sub-title: We take a look at the career of Philippe Coutinho, tracing his journey from a promising young talent to one of the Premier League’s most skilful and dynamic players. Delving into his passion, creativity, and legacy, we celebrate how his flair and vision have left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.
programme.desc: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.

programme.title: 1-TO-11 EP17: LUIS GARCIA
programme.sub-title: Liverpool and Spain star Luis Garcia selects his ultimate Premier League eleven, choosing from the best players he played alongside in England. Drawing on his experiences at Anfield and on the international stage, Garcia reflects on the talent, skill, and personalities that made his list. With insight, anecdotes and tough decisions, he builds his dream team from his unforgettable years in the Premier League.
programme.desc: Liverpool and Spain star Luis Garcia selects his ultimate Premier League eleven, choosing from the best players he played alongside in England. Drawing on his experiences at Anfield and on the international stage, Garcia reflects on the talent, skill, and personalities that made his list. With insight, anecdotes and tough decisions, he builds his dream team from his unforgettable years in the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP52: CHAMPIONS STORY
programme.sub-title: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United’s record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides—Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.
programme.desc: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP20: COLE PALMER
programme.sub-title: This week’s Big Interview sees two of Chelsea’s biggest stars from different generations come together for a football masterclass. Considered one of the most technically gifted players the Premier League has ever seen, Gianfranco Zola visits the Chelsea training ground to talk to the man currently lighting up the Premier League, Cole Palmer, about various aspects of his game. From free kicks to finishing, the pair watch and talk through clips of their play and then recreate those moments on the pitch. This one is not to be missed.

programme.title: PL MOMENTS EP09: GOODISON PARK
programme.sub-title: Former Everton midfielder Leon Osman celebrates the history of Goodison Park and selects the ten greatest Premier League moments to have happened at the iconic stadium
programme.desc: Former Everton midfielder Leon Osman celebrates the history of Goodison Park and selects the ten greatest Premier League moments to have happened at the iconic stadium

programme.title: PL STORIES EP51: GOODBYE GOODISON
programme.sub-title: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.
programme.desc: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP20: MCI v QPR 11/12
programme.sub-title: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.
programme.desc: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. programme.title: PL STORIES EP50: CHRIS WOOD
programme.sub-title: We follow New Zealand striker Chris Wood during a crucial World Cup Qualifier on home soil, at the peak of one of the most impressive seasons of his career. Back in his hometown, we meet the family, coaches and friends who helped shape the player and the person he’s become. From humble beginnings to Premier League mainstay, Wood reflects on the pressures he's faced, the pride of leading his country, and the mindset that's driven him every step of the way.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP20: MCI v QPR 11/12
programme.sub-title: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.
programme.desc: Manchester City v QPR on the final day of the 2011/12 season will forever be remembered as the most dramatic of finales to a Premier League season. Shaun Wright-Phillips and Joleon Lescott offer the perspective from both sides, with the final day drama at the Etihad set in the context of Manchester City’s Run-In and their pursuit of a first Premier League Title.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP51: GOODBYE GOODISON
programme.sub-title: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.
programme.desc: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP52: CHAMPIONS STORY
programme.sub-title: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United’s record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides—Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.
programme.desc: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP01: PAUL SCHOLES - MY GREATEST RIVALRY
programme.sub-title: One of the greatest players of his generation revisits some of the iconic Manchester United v Liverpool clashes that he was a part of. We hear the stories behind the images from the greatest rivalry in English football, with Scholes reminiscing about the many battles he fought in the middle of the park against Steven Gerrard.
programme.desc: One of the greatest players of his generation revisits some of the iconic Manchester United v Liverpool clashes that he was a part of. programme.title: PL STORIES EP48: (Subbed) REGGAE RHYTHMS IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE
programme.sub-title: Reggae Rhythms in the Premier League explores the powerful connection between Jamaica and the world’s most-watched football league, showcasing how the island’s culture, music, and style have influenced the Premier League. We feature iconic Jamaican players who have left their mark, while also diving into the impact of reggae music, fashion, and fan culture on the game. Through exclusive interviews and untold stories, we see how Jamaican heritage has shaped both football and broader global culture, from the pitch to the streets.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP42: CHAMPIONS SPECIAL WITH CURTIS JONES
programme.sub-title: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.
programme.desc: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.

programme.sub-title: We catch up with Juan Mata in Australia, where the former Premier League star is now playing with Western Sydney Wanderers. The Spaniard reflects on his memorable years in England with Chelsea and Manchester United, sharing insights into his greatest moments, personal growth, and connection with fans. programme.title: PL STORIES EP46: (Subbed) PHILIPPE COUTINHO
programme.sub-title: We take a look at the career of Philippe Coutinho, tracing his journey from a promising young talent to one of the Premier League’s most skilful and dynamic players. We join Coutinho at his current club Vasco da Gama and reflect on his time in Europe, from his rise at Liverpool to his time at Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Aston Villa. Delving into his passion, creativity, and legacy, we celebrate how his flair and vision have left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP20: COLE PALMER
programme.sub-title: This week’s Big Interview sees two of Chelsea’s biggest stars from different generations come together for a football masterclass. Considered one of the most technically gifted players the Premier League has ever seen, Gianfranco Zola visits the Chelsea training ground to talk to the man currently lighting up the Premier League, Cole Palmer, about various aspects of his game. From free kicks to finishing, the pair watch and talk through clips of their play and then recreate those moments on the pitch. This one is not to be missed.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP47: (Subbed) MO SALAH
programme.sub-title: We look back on the extraordinary career of Mohamed Salah, tracing his rise from a small village in Egypt to global football stardom. We speak to those closest to him to highlight his early years, struggles, and determination, shedding light on the pivotal moments that shaped his journey to becoming a Premier League legend with Liverpool.
programme.desc: We look back on the extraordinary career of Mohamed Salah, tracing his rise from a small village in Egypt to global football stardom. programme.title: PL STORIES EP52: CHAMPIONS STORY
programme.sub-title: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United’s record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides—Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.

Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.

programme.sub-title: We talk to the cast of characters who helped Blackpool reach the dizzy heights of the Premier League in the 2010-11 season. programme.title: PL STORIES EP06: DIDIER DROGBA HALL OF FAME SPECIAL
programme.sub-title: He scored 104 Premier League goals, making 55 assists in 254 English top flight matches for Chelsea. It’s no wonder then that four time Premier League winner Didier Drogba has earned a seat amongst the top table elite, after being inducted into the Premier League Hall of Fame. We talk to him and those that knew him best about what set him apart from others in the game, reflecting on his incredible career and his lasting legacy.
programme.desc: He scored 104 Premier League goals, making 55 assists in 254 English top flight matches for Chelsea. It’s no wonder then that four time Premier League winner Didier Drogba has earned a seat amongst the top table elite, after being inducted into the Premier League Hall of Fame. We talk to him and those that knew him best about what set him apart from others in the game, reflecting on his incredible career and his lasting legacy.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP04: JOHN TERRY HALL OF FAME SPECIAL
programme.sub-title: Considered a legend at Stamford Bridge, John Terry won no fewer than five League titles at Chelsea as well as lifting the F.A. Cup five times and the Champions League trophy. We find out what set him apart from his peers and where he rates amongst the very best in the World.
programme.desc: Considered a legend at Stamford Bridge, John Terry won no fewer than five League titles at Chelsea as well as lifting the F.A. Cup five times and the Champions League trophy. We find out what set him apart from his peers and where he rates amongst the very best in the World.
